items.headline: Cooling Inflation Boosts Sentiment, Geopolitical Tensions Add Caution
items.summary: Global stock markets are experiencing a mixed day. Positive momentum is largely driven by weaker-than-expected US inflation data, which has significantly reduced fears of immediate Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. However, renewed geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran, particularly the US blockade on Iranian ports, are pushing crude oil prices higher and reviving stagflation concerns, adding a layer of caution to investor sentiment.

items.headline: Aehr Test Systems (AEHR) Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) surged 30% after the company reported an impressive Q4 earnings beat, including 33% year-over-year revenue growth and robust FY2027 revenue guidance that significantly exceeded consensus estimates.

items.headline: PayPal (PYPL) Jumps on Acquisition Report
items.summary: PayPal ($PYPL) shares climbed 16% following reports that Stripe ($STRIP) and Advent International made a joint acquisition offer of $60.50 per share, valuing PayPal at over $53 billion and representing a 28% premium.

items.headline: SK Hynix (SKHY) Continues AI-Driven Rally
items.summary: SK Hynix ($SKHY) ADRs soared 27.47% on Nasdaq, extending gains seen in Seoul. The surge is attributed to positive U.S. tech stock performance after cooler inflation data and strong analyst outlooks for AI memory demand.

items.headline: IBM (IBM) Plunges on Weak Guidance, AI Shift
items.summary: International Business Machines ($IBM) shares plummeted 25.21% in its worst day since 1987. The sharp decline followed weaker-than-expected preliminary Q2 results and revenue guidance, as enterprise spending reportedly shifts towards AI infrastructure.

items.headline: US Inflation Cools, Easing Fed Rate Hike Concerns
items.summary: The US Consumer Price Index (CPI) unexpectedly fell 0.4% month-over-month in June, pushing the annual inflation rate down to 3.5%. This softer-than-expected reading significantly lowered market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate hike in July, with probabilities dropping from 42% to 17%.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Renewed US-Iran tensions and the re-imposed US naval blockade of Iranian ports have sent Brent crude oil prices soaring near $85-$86 per barrel. The escalating conflict revives stagflation fears and contributes to rising global bond yields, creating a challenging environment for equity markets.

items.headline: ASML's Strong Earnings Boost Semiconductor Sector
items.summary: Dutch chip equipment maker ASML Holding ($ASML) reported stronger-than-expected revenue and raised its financial forecasts for 2026. These results reinforce optimism about continued investment in AI infrastructure and advanced semiconductor production, providing a lift to the technology sector.

items.headline: Major US Banks Report Strong Quarterly Profits
items.summary: Five of the largest US banks, including Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, and Bank of America, posted a combined 39% jump in quarterly earnings. Strong investment banking fees and equities trading revenue buoyed these results, boosting investor confidence in the financial sector.

items.headline: China's Economic Growth Slows in Second Quarter
items.summary: China's economy expanded at a 4.3% annualized pace in the second quarter, marking its slowest quarterly growth since the pandemic began. Weak domestic demand is cited as a primary factor weighing on overall momentum, contributing to a lagging performance for the Shanghai Composite.
